title: Electrical Energy and Electrical Power — Easy

questions.question: What is the SI unit of electrical power?
questions.explanation:
According to the sources, the watt (W) is the SI unit for power. The joule is the unit for energy.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Which formula correctly relates electrical power ($P$), current ($I$), and potential difference ($V$)?
questions.explanation:
Power is defined as the product of the current and the potential difference, derived from substituting energy and charge equations.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of energy?
questions.explanation:
The SI unit of energy is the joule (J). The kilowatt-hour is a larger unit of energy used specifically for commercial electricity bills.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: What is the definition of a kilowatt-hour (kWh)?
options: The energy transferred to an appliance of power $1\text{ kW}$ in $1\text{ hour}$
The power used by a $1000\text{ W}$ appliance in $1\text{ second}$
The total voltage supplied to a house in one month
The current flowing through a $1000\text{ }Omega$ resistor
questions.explanation:
The sources define the kilowatt-hour (kWh) as the unit of energy representing the amount transferred to a $1\text{ kW}$ appliance over the duration of $1\text{ hour}$.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: If a $12\text{ V}$ battery delivers a current of $2.0\text{ A}$ to a motor, what is the power supplied?
questions.explanation:
Using the formula $P = IV$, we calculate $12\text{ V} \times 2.0\text{ A} = 24\text{ W}$.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.
